title: graphorkis odontoglossa
intro: Graphorkis odontoglossa, also known as the "tongue orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Graphorkis odontoglossa often involve boiling the plant's roots and leaves in water to create a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
